data.content:
# Implementation Plan — Honor knots-e2e over claim boundary

## Approach

Add a machine-readable `--e2e` flag on `kno claim` (and `kno poll --claim`) that
swaps the per-claim "Complete exactly one workflow action, then stop" boundary
for an "E2E continuation" boundary. The continuation boundary explicitly
authorizes the agent to re-run `kno claim --e2e <id>` after each completion
command and continue across action boundaries until SHIPPED, BLOCKED, or
DEFERRED. Default `kno claim` (no flag) keeps today's safety wording byte-for-
byte. The `knots-e2e` skill becomes the documented entry point and emits the
flag on every claim invocation. JSON output gains an `"e2e"` boolean and a
`"workflow_boundary_kind"` string so agents can detect mode without parsing
markdown.

## File-level changes

### Source code

1. `src/cli_agent.rs` — extend `ClaimArgs` with `pub e2e: bool` (`--e2e`,
   help: "Authorize end-to-end execution: emit an E2E continuation boundary
   that lets the agent reclaim the next state after each completion."). Mirror
   on `PollArgs` (`--claim` path) so `kno poll --claim --e2e` works.
2. `src/poll_claim.rs` — propagate `e2e` through `claim_knot`, add `e2e: bool`
   to `PollResult`, and forward through `render_text`, `render_text_verbose`,
   `render_json`, `render_json_verbose` into `prompt::render_prompt*`.
3. `src/prompt.rs` —
   - Add `e2e: bool` to `render_prompt`, `render_prompt_verbose`,
     `render_prompt_json`, `render_prompt_json_verbose`, and
     `render_prompt_inner`.
   - Branch in `render_workflow_boundary(state, allows_child_claims, e2e)`:
     - `e2e=false` → today's wording, unchanged.
     - `e2e=true` → "## Workflow Boundary (E2E continuation)" listing:
       - explicit e2e authorization for this run;
       - run the listed completion command;
       - after success, immediately run `kno claim --e2e <id>` on the new
         state and repeat until SHIPPED, BLOCKED, or DEFERRED;
       - the one-action limit does not apply during this run;
       - the e2e authorization satisfies repo policy for terminal-state
         movement.
   - In JSON, surface `"e2e": true|false` and
     `"workflow_boundary_kind": "single_action" | "e2e_continuation"`.
4. `src/poll_claim/tests_gate_ext.rs` — pass `false` to existing
   `render_prompt` callsite and add an e2e branch test.
5. `src/write_dispatch/operation_map.rs` — `map_claim` forwards the new flag
   if it is part of `WriteOperation`; verify and update.

### Skills (baked via `include_str!` into `.claude/skills/` and
`.agents/skills/`)

6. `skills/knots_e2e.md` — switch every `kno claim <id>` example to
   `kno claim --e2e <id>` (and `kno claim --e2e <id> --lease <lease>` where a
   lease is passed). Add an "## Invocation precedence" section: when this
   skill is in use, the e2e claim flag is the controlling boundary; the
   boundary text emitted by `kno claim --e2e` overrides the ordinary
   one-action wording.
7. `skills/knots.md` — leave plain `kno claim <id>` (default boundary).
   Add a one-liner pointing readers at `knots-e2e` for end-to-end runs.

### Documentation

8. `README.md` — add a short "Running a knot end-to-end" subsection under
   Knots usage. Show the user-facing override sentence verbatim and explain
   the `--e2e` flag.
9. `CLAUDE.md` (project) — under "Tracking Workflow", add: "For end-to-end
   runs, invoke the `knots-e2e` skill — it claims with `--e2e`, which emits
   the E2E continuation boundary so the agent continues across action
   boundaries."

### Tests

10. `src/prompt_tests.rs` — new tests:
    - `e2e_boundary_replaces_one_action_text` — e2e prompt does NOT contain
      "Complete exactly one workflow action" and DOES contain the
      continuation phrase plus a `kno claim --e2e` reference.
    - `default_boundary_unchanged` — `e2e=false` still emits today's wording.
    - `json_output_exposes_e2e_field` — JSON has `"e2e": true` and
      `"workflow_boundary_kind": "e2e_continuation"`.
11. `tests/cli_skills.rs` and/or `tests/cli_loom_prompt_resolution.rs` — CLI
    integration test for `kno claim --e2e <id>` confirming the flag flows
    through and the boundary text changes.
12. `src/poll_claim/tests_gate_ext.rs` — cover both branches.

## Verification

1. `cargo test` covers all new and existing tests, including
   `prompt_tests.rs` and `tests/cli_skills.rs`.
2. `make sanity` (formatting, lint, tests, coverage) — required pre-push
   gate.
3. Manual smoke:
   - `kno new "test e2e knot" -d "..."` → multi-step.
   - `kno claim --e2e <id>` → confirm boundary mentions "E2E continuation"
     and authorizes `kno claim --e2e` again on next state.
   - `kno claim <id>` (no flag, on a different knot) → confirm one-action
     boundary text is unchanged.

## Risks and mitigations

- Agents that hard-coded recognition of the existing boundary text may not
  notice the new wording. Mitigation: surface `"e2e"` and
  `"workflow_boundary_kind"` in JSON so machine-readable detection works,
  and update the skill so the very command the user runs already encodes
  the override.
- A stray `--e2e` outside the skill could bypass one-action safety.
  Mitigation: scope the flag to the documented entry point (`knots-e2e`);
  default `kno claim` is unchanged.
- Terminal-state movement policy. Mitigation: e2e boundary text states the
  e2e authorization satisfies repo policy for terminal-state movement.
  Default boundary unchanged so non-e2e paths remain locked.
- Other tests assert on boundary text. Mitigation: grep for the sentence
  and update affected fixtures before changes land.

## Complexity

Small-to-medium, ~150–300 LOC including tests. Single PR. Touches a focused
area: claim args → poll_claim → prompt render → skill text. Respects Knots
limits (file < 500 lines, function < 100 lines, line < 100 cols).

## Dependencies

None on other knots. Knot is self-contained; the e2e skill rollout is part
of this knot's scope.

## Invariant adherence

Knot has no listed invariants. Design preserves the existing one-action
boundary as the default, satisfying the "do not weaken default claim
safety" constraint from the knot description.
